records.title: 120 Minutes and a Laptop: Minimalist Image-goal Navigation via Unsupervised Exploration and Offline RL
records.citation: Xiaoming Liu, Borong Zhang, Qingbiao Li, Steven Morad (2026). 120 Minutes and a Laptop: Minimalist Image-goal Navigation via Unsupervised Exploration and Offline RL. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.26441
records.summary: The prevailing paradigm for image-goal visual navigation often assumes access to large-scale datasets, substantial pretraining, and significant computational resources. In this work, we challenge this assumption. We show that we can collect a dataset, train an in-domain policy, and deploy it to the real world (1) in le...
assumptions: Goals are represented explicitly and can condition policy/value updates.
Environment is modeled as an MDP/POMDP with stationary dynamics during training.
Replay-based off-policy or actor-critic updates can be stabilized with target networks and optimizer tuning.
contributions: Provides method/benchmark evidence directly reusable for goal-conditioned curiosity-driven RL.
Supplies comparator lineage or evaluation protocol for reproducible ablations.
claims: Shows measurable effects on sample efficiency, exploration, or final return under the reported setup.
conclusions: Method is effective in reported tasks but sensitive to reward scaling and schedule design.
limitations: Hyperparameter sensitivity and benchmark-specific tuning remain material risks.
future_work: Improve robustness, generalization, and scheduler adaptivity across broader tasks.

evidence_atoms.content: Reports benchmark evidence for exploration and/or return improvement relevant to sparse/deceptive reward settings.

records.title: Contrastive Learning as Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning
records.citation: Benjamin Eysenbach, Tianjun Zhang, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Sergey Levine (2022). Contrastive Learning as Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2206.07568
records.summary: Recasts goal-conditioned RL as contrastive learning, deriving value-like objectives that improve goal-reaching with strong sample efficiency in online and offline settings.
assumptions: Goal states are observable or representable for contrastive pairing.
Replay trajectories cover positive and negative goal pairs sufficiently.
contributions: Provides a formal bridge between contrastive objectives and goal-conditioned value learning.
Demonstrates improved success rates on image-based and offline goal-reaching tasks.
claims: Contrastive RL can outperform non-contrastive goal-conditioned baselines in sample efficiency and final success.
conclusions: Contrastive representation learning can serve as the core RL objective instead of an auxiliary loss.
limitations: Performance depends on negative-sample quality and encoder capacity.
future_work: Extend to harder long-horizon sparse tasks and richer observation modalities.

evidence_atoms.content: Q(s,a,g) = log p(g|s,a) - log p(g)
Jointly optimize goal-conditioned policy and contrastive representation from replay trajectories.

records.title: Diversity is All You Need: Learning Skills without a Reward Function
records.citation: Benjamin Eysenbach, Abhishek Gupta, Julian Ibarz, Sergey Levine (2018). Diversity is All You Need: Learning Skills without a Reward Function. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.1802.06070
records.summary: Introduces DIAYN, a reward-free objective for diverse skill discovery that maximizes skill discriminability and policy entropy, enabling transferable exploration priors.
assumptions: Latent skill variable z can induce distinguishable state occupancy distributions.
Discriminator q_phi(z|s) can be trained stably from off-policy data.
contributions: Defines an unsupervised skill-learning objective grounded in mutual information maximization.
Shows learned skills accelerate downstream sparse-reward learning.
claims: Reward-free skill pretraining improves exploration and transfer relative to random pretraining.
conclusions: Mutual-information-based skill diversity provides reusable exploratory behaviors.
limitations: Skill usefulness depends on latent cardinality and discriminator quality.
future_work: Integrate discovered skills with explicit goal-conditioned control and hierarchical planning.
key_equations: J(\pi,q) = E_{z,s,a}[log q_phi(z|s) - log p(z)] + alpha H(\pi(\cdot|s,z))

evidence_atoms.content: r_z(s,a)=log q_phi(z|s)-log p(z)
Unsupervised skills improve downstream task learning speed.

records.title: Reinforcement Learning with Unsupervised Auxiliary Tasks
records.citation: Max Jaderberg, Volodymyr Mnih, Wojciech Marian Czarnecki, Tom Schaul, Joel Z Leibo, David Silver, Koray Kavukcuoglu (2016). Reinforcement Learning with Unsupervised Auxiliary Tasks. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.1611.05397
records.summary: Presents UNREAL, augmenting A3C with auxiliary control and prediction tasks to improve data efficiency and robustness in sparse-reward environments.
assumptions: Auxiliary pseudo-reward tasks shape representations helpful for extrinsic return optimization.
Shared encoder can optimize multiple losses without destabilizing policy learning.
contributions: Introduces a multi-task RL training framework with replayed auxiliary objectives.
Shows significant speed and robustness gains over baseline A3C on 3D navigation benchmarks.
claims: Auxiliary task learning accelerates convergence and improves final performance under sparse rewards.
conclusions: Joint optimization of extrinsic and auxiliary objectives yields better representations and policy quality.
limitations: Auxiliary-loss weighting requires tuning and may be environment-dependent.
future_work: Improve automatic weighting and transfer of auxiliary objectives across domains.
key_equations: L_total = L_A3C + lambda_VR L_VR + lambda_PC L_PC + lambda_RP L_RP

evidence_atoms.content: L_total = L_A3C + lambda_VR L_VR + lambda_PC L_PC + lambda_RP L_RP
Replay-biased training for reward prediction and value replay alongside on-policy updates.
